description_long: This object is a heavily weathered and partially destroyed airplane, with significant structural damage and missing sections. The fuselage is elongated and appears to be covered in graffiti and debris, suggesting it has been abandoned for some time. The wings are broken and jagged, with one wing almost entirely separated from the main body. The tail section is either missing or severely damaged, and the landing gear is not visible, possibly due to destruction or removal. The surface is rough, with visible rust, dirt, and plant overgrowth in some areas. The overall appearance is that of a derelict aircraft wreck, possibly used as an urban art installation or left as a ruin.
description: A derelict, graffiti-covered airplane wreck with broken wings and missing tail, showing heavy weathering and debris.
description_view_0: Front-on view showing the broken fuselage, graffiti, and a separated wing section to the right.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the elongated, damaged fuselage and the jagged remains of the wings.
description_view_2: Top-down view revealing the full extent of the wing separation and the scattered debris around the airplane.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile with more visible plant overgrowth and clearer view of the broken wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight, broken wings and no visible winglets; wings are jagged and partially detached.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engines present due to destruction; engine mounts may be implied on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Tail section is missing or destroyed; no vertical or horizontal stabilizer is visible.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely destroyed or removed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted cockpit, heavily damaged and possibly open to the elements; canopy or windows are broken or missing.
